project.name: opensre
project.description: Open-source SRE agent for automated incident investigation and root cause analysis. Connects 60+ observability/incident tools, runs LangGraph-driven agentic investigations, and ships scored synthetic RCA suites.

nodes.name: benchmark-readme.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that regenerates and commits the README benchmark section, running the project's benchmark target on a scheduled cadence.

nodes.name: celebrate-merged-pr.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that posts a celebration message on merged pull requests as a contributor-engagement gesture.

nodes.name: codeql.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub CodeQL static analysis workflow scanning Python sources for security vulnerabilities on schedule and PR events.

nodes.name: tracer-demo-prefect-ecs.yml
nodes.summary: Workflow that builds and deploys the Prefect-on-ECS tracer demo environment used for end-to-end RCA scenario validation.

nodes.name: trigger-k8s-alert.yml
nodes.summary: Manually-dispatched workflow that fires a synthetic Kubernetes alert into the running agent stack to exercise the live RCA path.

nodes.name: docker-compose.database.yml
nodes.summary: Local Postgres 16 + Adminer compose stack used by the agent and tests for the OpenSRE database with healthcheck-gated service ordering.

nodes.name: opensre-db
nodes.summary: Postgres 16 service for the local OpenSRE database, exposing port 5432 with a `pg_isready` healthcheck.

nodes.name: adminer
nodes.summary: Adminer 4 web UI service exposed on port 8080 for inspecting the local OpenSRE Postgres instance, gated on `opensre-db` health.

nodes.name: docker-compose.rabbitmq.yml
nodes.summary: Local RabbitMQ stack for integration testing — broker plus a fast publisher and a deliberately slow consumer that builds a backlog within seconds for the queue-depth tools.

nodes.name: rabbitmq
nodes.summary: Single-node RabbitMQ 3.13 broker with management plugin (ports 5672/15672) configured with the `/orders` vhost.

nodes.name: publisher
nodes.summary: Python container that publishes ~50 msg/s to `orders.process` for backlog-driven test scenarios.

nodes.name: slow_consumer
nodes.summary: Deliberately-slow RabbitMQ consumer (5 msg/s) used to guarantee a growing queue depth so the queue-monitoring tools have something to detect.

nodes.name: docker-compose.testing.yml
nodes.summary: Test database stack bringing up Postgres 16 (with pg_stat_statements) and MySQL 8 with verbose query logging on alternate ports for integration tests.

nodes.name: postgres-test
nodes.summary: Postgres 16 test database on port 5433 with `pg_stat_statements` and statement-level logging enabled for query-analysis tool tests.

nodes.name: mysql-test
nodes.summary: MySQL 8 test database on port 3307 configured with slow-query logging, general logging, and binlog so the SQL-tooling tests have rich diagnostic surfaces.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Operator README describing how to authenticate with Cloudflare and deploy the install-proxy Worker that fronts `install.opensre.com`.

nodes.name: index.mjs
nodes.summary: Cloudflare Worker that serves install.opensre.com — auto-detects shell from path/query/User-Agent and proxies the matching install.sh or install.ps1 from the GitHub raw URL with no-store cache headers.
nodes.languageNotes: ES module Worker exporting a default `fetch` handler — uses URL/Headers Web APIs, no Node-specific dependencies.

nodes.name: requestedShell
nodes.summary: Resolves which shell variant to serve for an incoming request by inspecting the `?shell=` query, then falling back to the User-Agent string. Defaults to `sh`.

nodes.name: targetScriptUrl
nodes.summary: Maps the request's URL pathname (and shell hint) to the upstream raw GitHub install script URL — `install.ps1`, `install.sh`, or auto-selected.

nodes.name: wrangler.jsonc
nodes.summary: Wrangler config binding the install-proxy Worker (entry `src/index.mjs`) to the `install.opensre.com` custom-domain route.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Top-level `app` package init that re-exports the SDK entrypoint `run_investigation` so callers can `from app import run_investigation`.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Alerts package init that re-exports `normalize_alert_payload` as the package's single public symbol.

nodes.name: normalize.py
nodes.summary: Canonical alert-payload normalizer that converts source-specific (Datadog/Grafana/etc.) alert fields into a stable in-memory shape so downstream graph nodes consume one format regardless of origin.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of `Any | None` typing and small private helpers (`_as_mapping`, `_to_text`, `_first_present`) to centralize coercion concerns away from the main normalizer.

nodes.name: _parse_tags
nodes.summary: Parses Datadog-style tags into a `dict[str, str]`, accepting comma-separated strings, key-value mappings, or iterables of `key:value` items.

nodes.name: _coerce_pid
nodes.summary: Coerces a heterogeneous PID value (int, integer-valued float, numeric string) into an `int | None`, rejecting non-integer numeric inputs.

nodes.name: normalize_alert_payload
nodes.summary: Main entry that maps a raw alert dict (with vendor-specific labels/annotations) into the canonical OpenSRE alert shape — title, severity, service, host, PID, tags, and metadata.

nodes.name: install.py
nodes.summary: Tiny entry-point script that records a one-time install_detected analytics event during package post-install hooks.

nodes.name: jwt_auth.py
nodes.summary: Async JWT verification core that fetches Clerk JWKS via httpx, caches keys per-issuer with a TTL + per-issuer asyncio lock, and validates signature, issuer, and required claims.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements an async-safe JWKS cache using a lazily created asyncio.Lock per event loop to avoid thundering-herd refresh.

nodes.name: JWTClaims
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass capturing the verified JWT claims (sub, organization, slug, email, name, issuer, exp, iat) with a from_payload factory that defensively reads dict fields.

nodes.name: AsyncJWKSCache
nodes.summary: Async, TTL-bounded cache of Clerk JWKS keyed by issuer; uses per-loop asyncio locks to serialize refreshes and httpx for non-blocking fetches.

nodes.name: verify_jwt_async
nodes.summary: Asynchronously verifies a JWT against Clerk's JWKS: extracts unverified claims, picks the right issuer, fetches the signing key, and decodes with full signature/issuer/exp checks.

nodes.name: verify_jwt
nodes.summary: Synchronous wrapper around verify_jwt_async that runs the async verifier on the active or a fresh event loop for callers that cannot await.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: CLI package init that re-exports support.args helpers so callers can do `from app.cli import ...` without reaching into submodules.

nodes.name: guardrails.py
nodes.summary: Thin Click wrappers for the guardrails subsystem (init, test, audit, list rules) that delegate to app.guardrails.cli.

nodes.name: onboard.py
nodes.summary: `onboard` command that runs the wizard for first-time setup, plus an `onboard local-llm` variant that configures a local model provider.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Interactive REPL barrel — re-exports `run_repl` from app.cli.interactive_shell.loop (callers use `from app.cli.interactive_shell import run_repl`).

nodes.name: terminal_intent.py
nodes.summary: Vocabulary-based intent detectors used by the router: alert signals, integration mentions, CLI-agent operational intent, and sample-alert launch shortcuts.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the investigation entry points (`run_investigation_cli`, streaming variants, session helpers) for the CLI layer.

nodes.name: alert_templates.py
nodes.summary: Builds canned sample alert payloads (Kubernetes, database, latency, etc.) used by the REPL's sample-alert launch flow and onboarding demos.

nodes.name: build_alert_template
nodes.summary: Returns a fully-formed sample alert payload string for the named template (e.g. `kubernetes`, `database`) for use as REPL/demo input.

nodes.name: investigate_input.py
nodes.summary: Interactive prompt helpers (`questionary`-driven) for picking sample alerts or selecting a payload file when no alert source is given on the CLI.

nodes.name: prompt_for_input
nodes.summary: Interactive entry: lets the user choose between sample alerts, a file path, or stdin to source the alert payload.

nodes.name: payload.py
nodes.summary: Loads alert payloads from file, stdin, interactive prompt, or raw text and parses them into the structured form expected by the investigation pipeline.

nodes.name: load_payload
nodes.summary: Top-level dispatcher that picks the correct payload source (file, raw JSON, or interactive prompt) based on CLI flags.

nodes.name: load_file
nodes.summary: Reads an alert payload file from disk with informative error handling for missing or unreadable files.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the local LLM (Ollama) onboarding subpackage.

nodes.name: command.py
nodes.summary: Implements the `opensre onboard local_llm` zero-config flow: detects hardware, installs/launches Ollama, recommends a model, pulls it, and writes the resulting provider config.

nodes.name: run_local_llm_setup
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full local-LLM onboarding sequence with Rich progress UI: hardware detection → Ollama install → server start → model pull → wizard config persistence.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for shared CLI support utilities (args, constants, context, errors, exit codes).

nodes.name: args.py
nodes.summary: Argparse wiring for the legacy CLI entry plus a `write_json` helper that serializes pipeline output to disk for `--json-output` mode.

nodes.name: parse_args
nodes.summary: Builds the argparse parser and parses CLI argv into a structured `Namespace` for the legacy `opensre` entrypoint.

nodes.name: context.py
nodes.summary: Click-context accessors for shared CLI flags (`--json-output`, `--verbose`, `--debug`, `--yes`) that propagate to nested commands.

nodes.name: exit_codes.py
nodes.summary: Named exit-code constants (success, generic failure, config error, network error) used consistently across CLI commands.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Wizard package barrel exposing a lazy `run_wizard` shim so that import-time side effects in `flow` only happen when the wizard actually runs.

nodes.name: env_sync.py
nodes.summary: Reads, mutates, and writes the user's `.env` file to keep wizard-selected provider, model, and credential values in sync without clobbering unrelated entries.

nodes.name: sync_env_values
nodes.summary: Merges a key/value mapping into the `.env` file, preserving order and comments while updating or appending the requested keys.

nodes.name: sync_provider_env
nodes.summary: Updates the `.env` file to reflect the selected LLM provider/model and removes leftover keys from previously selected providers.

nodes.name: grafana_seed.py
nodes.summary: Seeds the local Loki instance with synthetic pipeline and supporting log streams so the bundled Grafana stack has demo data immediately after launch.

nodes.name: wait_for_loki
nodes.summary: Polls the Loki ready endpoint until it responds healthy or a timeout elapses, returning a boolean readiness flag.

nodes.name: _pipeline_log_stream
nodes.summary: Builds a synthetic log stream representing a CI/CD pipeline (build, deploy, error) for Loki seeding.

nodes.name: _supporting_log_stream
nodes.summary: Builds supporting application/dependency log streams used alongside the pipeline stream when seeding Loki.

nodes.name: seed_logs
nodes.summary: Pushes the synthesized log streams to Loki via its `/loki/api/v1/push` endpoint.

nodes.name: integration_health.py
nodes.summary: Stable public surface that re-exports every onboarding validator (HTTP probe, MCP, client-based) plus the shared `IntegrationHealthResult` model.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the integration_validators sub-package.

nodes.name: http_probe_validators.py
nodes.summary: Plain-HTTP probe validators for integrations without a dedicated service client (Slack webhooks, Notion, Jira, Discord), each returning a normalized health result.

nodes.name: validate_slack_webhook
nodes.summary: Probes a Slack incoming-webhook URL with a no-op or test payload to verify reachability and authentication.

nodes.name: validate_notion_integration
nodes.summary: Validates a Notion integration token and database ID by issuing a query against the Notion API.

nodes.summary: Validates a Discord bot token by calling the `/users/@me` endpoint and checking for a bot identity.

nodes.name: mcp_validators.py
nodes.summary: MCP-server health validators for the GitHub MCP and OpenClaw integrations, supporting both HTTP and local stdio modes.

nodes.summary: Validates GitHub MCP connectivity in either remote (URL+token) or local (command/args) mode and reports toolset and repo visibility.

nodes.name: validate_openclaw_integration
nodes.summary: Validates OpenClaw MCP connectivity in either remote or local mode.

nodes.name: shared.py
nodes.summary: Shared `IntegrationHealthResult` dataclass returned by every validator in the integration_validators package.

nodes.name: IntegrationHealthResult
nodes.summary: Result struct returned by integration validators: `ok` flag, optional `detail` message, and optional GitHub-MCP-specific metadata.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Two-service docker-compose stack (Grafana 11.1 + Loki 3.0) used by the wizard's local-Grafana flow to give first-time users a working observability stack on `localhost:3000`.
nodes.languageNotes: Anonymous admin auth is enabled (GF_AUTH_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED=true) for friction-free local dev only.

nodes.name: loki
nodes.summary: Loki 3.0.0 log aggregation service exposed on port 3100, started with the bundled local-config.yaml.

nodes.name: grafana
nodes.summary: Grafana 11.1.0 service exposed on port 3000 with anonymous admin login enabled, depending on the loki service and consuming the bundled provisioning directory.

nodes.name: loki.yaml
nodes.summary: Grafana datasource provisioning file that pre-registers the bundled Loki instance as the default datasource.

nodes.name: probes.py
nodes.summary: Lightweight reachability probes used by the wizard to decide between local install (writable home) and remote runtime (Tracer cloud) targets.

nodes.name: probe_remote_target
nodes.summary: Probes the Tracer cloud base URL with a short HTTP timeout to determine whether remote runtime is reachable.

nodes.name: ProbeResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing target identifier, reachability flag, and human-readable detail for a single probe outcome.

nodes.name: prompts.py
nodes.summary: Custom prompt-toolkit-based `select` and `checkbox` widgets with grouping, hint rendering, and Escape-aware key bindings; replaces questionary defaults for a tighter UX.

nodes.name: select
nodes.summary: Single-select prompt widget supporting groups, hints, custom styles, and Escape-cancellation, returning either the chosen value or a sentinel on cancel.

nodes.name: checkbox
nodes.summary: Multi-select checkbox prompt with grouping, hint rendering, and toggle key bindings.

nodes.name: _base_bindings
nodes.summary: Builds the shared key-binding registry (arrows, Enter, Escape, optional toggle) used by both `select` and `checkbox` prompts.

nodes.name: _CheckboxControl
nodes.summary: Custom prompt-toolkit `UIControl` that renders checkbox tokens with group headings and hover/check styling.

nodes.name: store.py
nodes.summary: JSON-backed store for wizard-derived configuration: local config (provider/model/probes), remote URLs, named remotes, active remote tracking, and remote ops settings, all under `~/.opensre`.

nodes.name: save_local_config
nodes.summary: Persists the wizard's local configuration (mode, provider, model, env-var names, probe results) to the JSON store atomically.

nodes.name: save_named_remote
nodes.summary: Adds or updates a named remote entry, optionally marks it active, and records its source for later auditing.

nodes.name: set_active_remote
nodes.summary: Switches the active remote pointer to a previously saved named remote, validating its existence.

nodes.name: save_remote_ops_config
nodes.summary: Persists the remote-operations configuration (provider, project, service) used by remote runtime targets.

nodes.name: _load_raw
nodes.summary: Loads and parses the on-disk JSON store, returning an empty dict if the file is missing or malformed.

nodes.name: load_remote_ops_config
nodes.summary: Reads the remote-operations sub-section from the local store, returning a dict with provider/project/service entries.

nodes.name: posthog.py
nodes.summary: Centralized PostHog analytics constants: capture API key, host, default URL, request timeout, and bounce-rate thresholds.

nodes.name: prompts.py
nodes.summary: Defines the system, general, and router prompt templates that drive the Tracer chat agent's persona and routing logic between data-backed and conversational modes.

nodes.name: __init__.py

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel for the deployment-methods subpackage that re-exports the LangSmith and Railway shipping helpers.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel for deployment runtime operations re-exporting EC2 outputs, health-poll helpers, and provider config validators.

nodes.name: ec2_config.py

nodes.name: save_remote_outputs
nodes.summary: Serializes the managed EC2 deployment outputs to JSON and persists them to the local user state directory.

nodes.name: load_remote_outputs

nodes.name: health.py

nodes.name: poll_deployment_health

nodes.name: provider_config.py

nodes.name: ProviderValidationResult
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass holding the provider name, ok flag, error tuple, and normalized config dict produced by a validator.

nodes.name: validate_aws_deploy_config

nodes.name: validate_vercel_deploy_config

nodes.name: validate_railway_deploy_config

nodes.name: dry_run_provider_validation

nodes.name: cmd_rules

nodes.name: resolve_effective_integrations

nodes.name: _build_summary_prompt

nodes.name: base.py

nodes.languageNotes: Uses pydantic StrictConfigModel and ABC to enforce metadata declared as ClassVars on subclasses, with validation in __init_subclass__.

nodes.name: ToolMetadata
nodes.summary: Strict pydantic schema describing the metadata every BaseTool subclass must declare, including name, description, input schema, evidence source, and retrieval controls.

nodes.name: BaseTool
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for class-based tools; declares metadata as ClassVars, validates them via ToolMetadata in __init_subclass__, and exposes is_available/extract_params/run hooks.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package facade that re-exports the investigation action registry API: action listing, prioritization helpers, and the InvestigationAction alias.

nodes.name: actions.py
nodes.summary: Thin adapter that exposes get_available_actions(), returning the investigation-surface RegisteredTool snapshot from the canonical tool registry.

nodes.name: models.py
nodes.summary: Compatibility shim that aliases the legacy InvestigationAction name to the canonical RegisteredTool dataclass.

nodes.name: prioritization.py
nodes.summary: Ranks the available investigation actions for a given alert by scoring each tool's source/use-cases against alert keywords and integration availability.

nodes.name: get_prioritized_actions_with_reasons
nodes.summary: Scores each available action against alert keywords and configured sources, returning the ranked actions with human-readable reasons.

nodes.name: registered_tool.py
nodes.summary: Defines RegisteredTool, the canonical runtime tool dataclass shared by class-based and function-based tools, with factories from BaseTool subclasses or decorated functions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses dataclass + typing.get_type_hints to infer JSON Schemas from Python annotations, supporting Optional unwrapping and Literal/list/dict mapping.

nodes.name: _normalize_surfaces
nodes.summary: Validates and de-duplicates a tool's surface tuple (e.g. investigation, chat), raising on unknown surfaces.

nodes.name: _annotation_to_json_schema
nodes.summary: Maps a Python type annotation (str, int, list, dict, Literal, Optional) to a JSON Schema fragment used for tool input schemas.

nodes.name: infer_input_schema
nodes.summary: Inspects a function's signature and type hints to synthesize a JSON Schema describing required and optional parameters.

nodes.name: RegisteredTool
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass that holds the runtime metadata, callable, surfaces, and factories for both BaseTool subclasses and decorated function tools.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: Canonical tool registry that walks the app.tools package via pkgutil, collects RegisteredTool instances, and exposes cached lookups by surface (investigation/chat).
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pkgutil.iter_modules for runtime discovery and functools.lru_cache to memoize the registry snapshot per surface.

nodes.name: _collect_registered_tools_from_module
nodes.summary: Inspects an imported tool module for BaseTool subclasses or decorated functions and yields their RegisteredTool representations.

nodes.name: _load_registry_snapshot
nodes.summary: Iterates discoverable tool modules, imports each, and assembles the deduplicated tuple of RegisteredTool entries that backs the registry cache.

nodes.name: get_registered_tools
nodes.summary: Public API returning all RegisteredTool entries filtered to a given surface (investigation or chat).

nodes.name: get_registered_tool_map
nodes.summary: Public API returning a name-keyed dict of RegisteredTool entries for the requested surface.

nodes.name: resolve_tool_display_name
nodes.summary: Looks up the human-readable display name for a registered tool by its canonical name, falling back to the canonical name if no override exists.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel module that re-exports tool utilities: code-host helpers, evidence compaction, log deduplication, data validation, and database warnings.

nodes.name: availability.py
nodes.summary: Helpers that check whether EKS, Datadog, or CloudWatch integrations are configured for a given sources mapping, used by tool is_available hooks.

nodes.name: code_host_unavailable.py
nodes.summary: Returns a standard placeholder payload signaling that a code-host integration (GitHub, GitLab, etc.) is not configured for the current investigation.

nodes.name: data_validation.py
nodes.summary: Host-metrics validator that flags impossible or suspicious values (e.g. memory percentages over 100, units mismatches) and emits structured ValidationIssue records.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a pydantic-style ValidationIssue dataclass plus a stateful MetricsValidator class that mutates a shared issues list across heuristic checks.

nodes.name: ValidationIssue
nodes.summary: Immutable record describing a single validation issue: field, raw value, type, severity, explanation, and suggested fix.

nodes.name: MetricsValidator
nodes.summary: Heuristic validator for host metrics that checks memory, CPU, disk, and flat metrics for impossible percentages, unit mismatches, and inconsistencies, returning ValidationIssue records.

nodes.name: validate_host_metrics
nodes.summary: Public entry point that runs MetricsValidator over a metrics payload and returns the metrics enriched with any validation issues detected.

nodes.name: db_warnings.py
nodes.summary: Provides default_db_warning, a tiny helper that returns a standard cautionary message recommending verification before acting on database evidence.

nodes.name: eks_workload_helper.py
nodes.summary: Shared EKS helper utilities that extract AWS credentials, workload identifiers (cluster/namespace/workload), and cluster parameters from heterogeneous source dicts feeding the EKS tools.

nodes.name: extract_workload_params
nodes.summary: Extracts cluster_name, namespace, and workload_name from a list of source dicts, normalizing keys for downstream EKS tool calls.

nodes.name: extract_cluster_params
nodes.summary: Extracts cluster_name and region from heterogeneous source dicts so EKS cluster-level tools can run with consistent parameters.

nodes.name: log_compaction.py
nodes.summary: Log post-processing utilities that deduplicate noisy log lines, classify error types, and build a compact taxonomy with sample messages so the LLM-facing tool outputs stay within token budgets.
nodes.languageNotes: Heuristic message normalization strips IDs, timestamps, IPs, and UUIDs to collapse near-duplicate log lines into a single bucket.

nodes.name: deduplicate_logs
nodes.summary: Collapses near-duplicate log entries by normalized message into representative samples, keeping count/timespan metadata while honoring an output cap.

nodes.name: build_error_taxonomy
nodes.summary: Groups logs into an error-type taxonomy with extracted components and representative sample messages to give the LLM a structured view of failure modes.

nodes.name: compact_logs
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point that runs deduplication and taxonomy generation to produce a compact, LLM-friendly representation of a log batch.

nodes.name: _extract_components
nodes.summary: Heuristically extracts service/component identifiers from log messages to enrich taxonomy buckets with origin information.

nodes.name: retrieval.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic models describing structured retrieval intents (time bounds, filters, limits, field selection, aggregation) and per-tool support metadata used by the planner to translate LLM-issued queries into concrete tool invocations.

nodes.name: TimeBounds
nodes.summary: Pydantic model expressing a query time window via absolute start/end timestamps or a relative lookback in minutes.

nodes.name: FilterCondition
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for a single field/operator/value filter applied to a retrieval query.

nodes.name: FieldSelection
nodes.summary: Pydantic model describing which fields to include or exclude from a retrieval result set.

nodes.name: AggregationSpec
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for aggregation queries (group_by, function, optional field, time bucket) with a validator enforcing that non-count aggregations specify a field.

nodes.name: RetrievalIntent
nodes.summary: Composite Pydantic model bundling time bounds, filters, limits, field selection, and aggregation into a single retrieval plan with a has_controls helper.

nodes.name: RetrievalControls
nodes.summary: Per-tool capability metadata describing which retrieval controls (time, filters, limits, fields, aggregation) a given tool actually supports, with helpers to query support flags.

nodes.name: tools.py
nodes.summary: Defines ToolSurface, the Literal type enumerating where a tool may appear (investigation pipeline, chat surface, etc.).

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the cross-cutting app.utils module — utility submodules are imported directly by their callers.

nodes.name: cfg_helpers.py
nodes.summary: CfgHelpers static class with environment-variable lookups and LLM-provider resolution helpers used by the configuration layer.

nodes.name: CfgHelpers
nodes.summary: Static helper namespace bundling first_env_or_default, resolve_llm_provider, and get_clean_env_value for ergonomic config resolution.

nodes.name: coercion.py
nodes.summary: Tiny coercion helpers; currently exposes safe_int that returns a default when integer parsing fails.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Environment-driven configuration helpers covering Grafana Cloud account credentials, OTLP/OTel endpoints and protocols, hosted log/metric/trace IDs, and resource attribute construction.
nodes.languageNotes: Module is organized as 30+ small accessor functions over os.environ rather than a Pydantic settings class, keeping the env-var contract explicit and testable.

nodes.name: load_env
nodes.summary: Loads a .env file into the process environment with optional override of existing variables.

nodes.name: configure_grafana_cloud
nodes.summary: Writes Grafana Cloud OTLP credentials and endpoint configuration into the supplied env file so subsequent processes can authenticate.

nodes.name: apply_otel_env_defaults
nodes.summary: Applies sensible OpenTelemetry exporter defaults (protocol, endpoint, headers) to the environment when not already set.

nodes.name: validate_grafana_cloud_config
nodes.summary: Validates that the Grafana Cloud OTLP environment is internally consistent (matching protocol, endpoint, and credentials).

nodes.name: get_account_datasource_uids
nodes.summary: Returns the list of Grafana datasource UIDs configured for a given account ID via environment variables.

nodes.name: list_account_ids
nodes.summary: Discovers all Grafana account IDs declared in the environment by scanning known prefixed env-var patterns.

nodes.name: discord_delivery.py
nodes.summary: Discord delivery utilities that authenticate as a bot, post messages with embeds, create threads, and emit redacted error info — used to publish RCA reports to Discord channels.

nodes.name: post_discord_message
nodes.summary: Posts a message with optional embeds to a Discord channel using the Discord bot REST API and returns the parsed delivery response.

nodes.name: create_discord_thread
nodes.summary: Creates a Discord thread anchored to an existing channel message so follow-up RCA discussion has a dedicated thread.

nodes.name: send_discord_report
nodes.summary: High-level helper that composes a Discord embed payload from an RCA report and sends it via post_discord_message.

nodes.name: slack_delivery.py
nodes.summary: Slack delivery layer covering reaction add/remove/swap, action-button block construction, and posting reports via three transport modes — direct bot token, hosted webapp relay, and incoming webhook.
nodes.languageNotes: Three coexistent posting paths (_post_direct, _post_via_webapp, _post_via_incoming_webhook) let the same report code work in self-hosted, hosted, and webhook-only deployments.

nodes.name: add_reaction
nodes.summary: Adds an emoji reaction to a Slack message via reactions.add, used for status-change feedback (e.g., investigating, resolved).

nodes.name: remove_reaction
nodes.summary: Removes an emoji reaction from a Slack message via reactions.remove.

nodes.name: swap_reaction
nodes.summary: Atomically replaces one Slack reaction with another to signal a status transition on the source message.

nodes.name: build_action_blocks
nodes.summary: Builds the Slack Block Kit action blocks (View Investigation button, etc.) attached to RCA report messages.

nodes.name: send_slack_report
nodes.summary: Top-level Slack report sender that picks the right transport (direct, webapp, or incoming webhook) based on configured credentials and posts the report blocks.

nodes.name: _post_direct
nodes.summary: Posts a Slack message using a direct bot token via chat.postMessage with redacted error reporting.

nodes.name: _post_via_webapp
nodes.summary: Posts a Slack message by relaying through the hosted webapp Slack proxy when no direct bot token is configured locally.

nodes.name: _post_via_incoming_webhook
nodes.summary: Posts a Slack message via an incoming webhook URL when neither bot token nor webapp relay is available.

nodes.name: telegram_delivery.py
nodes.summary: Telegram bot delivery utilities that post messages with reply threading, redact bot tokens from logs, and install a logging filter to keep tokens out of httpx logs.

nodes.name: post_telegram_message
nodes.summary: Posts a message to a Telegram chat via the Bot API with optional reply threading and parse mode, redacting tokens from any error output.

nodes.name: send_telegram_report
nodes.summary: High-level helper that formats an RCA report as Telegram-flavored text and sends it via post_telegram_message.

nodes.name: truncation.py
nodes.summary: Tiny string-truncation helper used by delivery formatters to keep payloads under integration-specific length limits.

nodes.name: url_validation.py
nodes.summary: URL validation helpers that allow only HTTPS, or HTTP for loopback hosts, used to harden user-supplied integration endpoints.

nodes.name: validate_https_or_loopback_http_url
nodes.summary: Raises a descriptive validation error when a service URL is neither HTTPS nor an HTTP loopback address, used by integration config validators.

nodes.name: version.py
nodes.summary: Exposes get_version which reads the installed package version (or a fallback) so the CLI, web health endpoint, and Sentry release tagging share a single source of truth.

nodes.name: get_version
nodes.summary: Returns the installed opensre package version via importlib.metadata, falling back to a default string when not packaged.

nodes.name: bug_report.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub issue form template for bug reports collecting reproduction steps, environment, and severity from contributors.

nodes.name: config.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub issue chooser configuration that disables blank issues and provides links to docs and discussion channels.

nodes.name: improvement.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub issue template scaffolding the structured form for filing improvement requests against the opensre repository.

nodes.name: PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md
nodes.summary: Pull request template prompting contributors for description, testing notes, and the four checklist items required before review.

nodes.name: codeql-config.yml
nodes.summary: CodeQL scanner configuration restricting static-analysis paths/queries used by the GitHub security workflow.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore manifest excluding generated artifacts, fixtures, and large binaries from the understand-anything knowledge graph scan.

nodes.name: domain-graph.json
nodes.summary: Persisted business-domain knowledge graph derived by the understand-anything plugin, capturing top-level domains and flow relationships.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Single-line indirection that imports AGENTS.md and CLAUDE_PERSONAL.md so Claude Code inherits the same instructions as other agents.

nodes.name: DEPLOYMENT.md
nodes.summary: Operator-facing guide describing how to deploy opensre to LangGraph Platform and Railway, including environment, build, and verification steps.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Production container image — installs the agent via `pip install --no-cache-dir -c /api/constraints.txt /deps/agent` (NON-editable) and exposes the LangGraph runtime via LANGSERVE_GRAPHS pointing at app/graph_pipeline.py:build_graph.
nodes.languageNotes: Sets LANGSERVE_GRAPHS to mount app/graph_pipeline.py:graph as the 'agent' graph; HEALTHCHECK uses /ok endpoint with 30s interval.

nodes.name: langgraph-api
nodes.summary: Single-stage container service built on langchain/langgraph-api:3.11 that publishes the opensre LangGraph agent on TCP 2024.

nodes.name: langgraph.json
nodes.summary: LangGraph deployment manifest — declares `auth.path`, `http.app`, `image_distro`, and the `env` file plus the graph entry under `graphs`. Consumed by LangGraph Platform / langgraph deploy.

nodes.name: mypy.ini
nodes.summary: mypy configuration — strict typing baseline plus per-module overrides for third-party libraries that lack type stubs (boto3/botocore/kubernetes/requests/questionary).

nodes.name: ruff.toml
nodes.summary: Ruff linter and formatter configuration controlling line length, enabled rule sets, and per-path ignores for the codebase.

nodes.name: get_available_actions
nodes.summary: Return the list of investigation-surface RegisteredTool entries by delegating to ``get_registered_tools('investigation')``; thin facade exposing the investigation slice of the auto-discovered tool registry.

nodes.name: route_by_mode
nodes.summary: Top-level mode router for the unified chat/investigation graph; returns the next-node label based on AgentState mode.

nodes.name: route_chat
nodes.summary: Routes within chat mode; returns 'tools' when the model produced tool_calls, otherwise 'end'.

nodes.name: route_after_extract
nodes.summary: Routes after the extract_alert node; returns 'investigate' if extraction succeeded, otherwise terminal.

nodes.name: route_investigation_loop
nodes.summary: Loop controller for the planner→action→diagnose cycle; decides continue/exit based on state.investigation_loop_count and verdict.

nodes.name: should_call_tools
nodes.summary: Tool-call gate for the chat mode; returns 'tools' if the latest AIMessage contains pending tool_calls, else 'end'.

nodes.name: merge_results_reducer
nodes.summary: LangGraph reducer for the parallel hypothesis_results channel; supports a __clear sentinel to reset and otherwise concatenates incoming lists.

nodes.name: save_remote_url
nodes.summary: Persist the active remote URL into the local wizard config (single-active-remote shape).

nodes.name: load_named_remotes
nodes.summary: Load the {name → url} dict of named remotes from the local wizard config.

nodes.name: load_active_remote_name
nodes.summary: Return the currently-active remote name set via set_active_remote, or None.

nodes.name: clear_tool_registry_cache
nodes.summary: Drop the cached registry snapshot — used by tests to force re-discovery via _iter_tool_module_names + pkgutil.iter_modules.

nodes.name: RemoteOpsError
nodes.summary: Base RuntimeError raised by remote-ops provider operations; imported by app/cli/commands/remote.py for user-facing error translation.

nodes.name: RemoteServiceScope
nodes.summary: @dataclass scope descriptor (provider/service/environment) — required argument on every RemoteOpsProvider method.

nodes.name: VercelResolutionError
nodes.summary: RuntimeError raised when Vercel project/URL/integration resolution fails; translated to HTTP 400 by remote/server.py investigate-stream handlers.

nodes.name: get_grafana_client
nodes.summary: Env-based GrafanaClient factory — resolves credentials from env vars (the canonical entry point used outside the credential-store flow).

nodes.name: extract_org_id_from_jwt
nodes.summary: Decode an unverified JWT and return its org_id claim (used by tracer_client to propagate org context across remote calls).

nodes.name: get_remote_outputs_path
nodes.summary: Return the local path where EC2 deployment-output files are persisted.

nodes.name: delete_remote_outputs
nodes.summary: Delete the persisted EC2 deployment-output files (cleanup helper called from the CLI on `opensre deploy clean`).

nodes.name: feature_request.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub issue template for feature requests, prompting contributors to describe the problem statement and proposed solution.

nodes.name: dependabot.yml
nodes.summary: Dependabot configuration scheduling weekly uv-managed Python dependency updates and daily npm updates for the docs site.

nodes.name: ci-labels-windows.yml
nodes.summary: Optional Windows CI workflow gated on the `ci:windows` PR label, running the quality suite on windows-latest in an isolated concurrency group.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses label-conditional execution to keep the Windows runner opt-in and avoid blocking every PR on slow Windows jobs.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: Primary CI workflow that runs lint, typecheck, format-check, and the unit/coverage test suite across the matrix on push and PR to main.
nodes.languageNotes: Path-filtered triggers limit runs to changes that actually affect Python sources, tests, build config, or the workflow itself.

nodes.name: contributors.yml
nodes.summary: Workflow that regenerates the contributors list weekly and on push to main, committing updates back to the repository.

nodes.name: good-first-issue-assign.yml
nodes.summary: Auto-assigns the first eligible new contributor who comments on an open `good first issue`, gating on prior PR count and existing assignees.

nodes.name: greptile-pr-reminder.yml
nodes.summary: Posts a one-time Greptile code review reminder comment when a PR is opened or reopened, using pull_request_target so it works for forks safely.
nodes.languageNotes: Avoids checking out the PR head to mitigate the standard pull_request_target privilege-escalation risk.

nodes.name: release-announcements.yml
nodes.summary: Fans out release announcements to Discord when a GitHub release is published, gated to the upstream non-fork repository only.

nodes.name: release.yml
nodes.summary: Core release pipeline that builds, tags, and publishes opensre on push to main, via nightly cron, or manual dispatch with channel selection.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses dynamic concurrency keys per channel so stable and main-channel publishes do not cancel each other.

nodes.name: fetch_opensre_hub_alert.py
nodes.summary: Standalone script that streams OpenRCA-style alert JSON from the Hugging Face OpenSRE dataset, supporting single-alert fetch and batch export.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Argparse entry point that resolves prefix/index/limit options and writes one or many streamed alerts to disk or stdout.

nodes.name: query_opensre_telemetry.py
nodes.summary: Local CLI for querying OpenSRE/OpenRCA telemetry CSVs (list, raw, metrics, logs, traces) without a Grafana server, mirroring what node_investigate runs during evidence gathering.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Argparse top-level dispatcher routing list/raw/metrics/logs/traces subcommands to their respective handler functions.

nodes.name: _resolve_telemetry_dir
nodes.summary: Resolves the telemetry directory from explicit --telemetry-dir, a local clone root plus relative path, or by materializing the folder from the Hugging Face Hub.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Analytics package barrel that re-exports the Event enum, Analytics provider, Properties type alias, and global get/shutdown helpers.

nodes.name: cli.py
nodes.summary: PostHog event capture facade for the CLI, exposing dozens of typed `capture_*` helpers for onboarding, investigation, integrations, tests, deploy, and update lifecycles.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses small `_bucket_*` helpers to coarsen high-cardinality numeric properties before sending, keeping PostHog event schemas stable.

nodes.name: build_cli_invoked_properties
nodes.summary: Builds the property bag for the cli_invoked event from the resolved Click command path and supplied argv tokens.

nodes.name: capture_terminal_turn_summarized
nodes.summary: Captures the interactive terminal turn-summary event with bucketed durations and rubric metrics for evaluation analytics.

nodes.name: capture_eval_process_completed
nodes.summary: Captures completion of an eval process with bucketed scores and percentages so dashboards see stable categorical distributions.

nodes.name: events.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Event StrEnum that names every analytics event across CLI lifecycle, onboarding, investigation, integrations, tests, evals, terminal, deploy, and update.

nodes.name: Event
nodes.summary: StrEnum cataloging every analytics event identifier emitted by the CLI, grouped by lifecycle, onboarding, investigation, integrations, tests, evals, terminal, deploy, and update.
nodes.languageNotes: StrEnum keeps event values usable as plain strings while preserving type-safety for callers.

nodes.name: provider.py
nodes.summary: Background-threaded PostHog analytics transport managing anonymous identity, atomic ID persistence, composite fingerprinting, queueing, retry-safe shutdown, event log rotation, and failure logging.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines a single-writer queue worker with file locks and atomic writes to keep anonymous IDs stable across concurrent CLI invocations.

nodes.name: Analytics
nodes.summary: Process-wide analytics client that captures events through a bounded queue and a daemon worker, with opt-out detection, atexit-driven shutdown, and graceful drain semantics.

nodes.name: _compute_anonymous_identity
nodes.summary: Resolves a stable anonymous distinct ID by reading the persisted UUID, migrating legacy IDs, or atomically writing a new one under file lock.

nodes.name: _build_composite_fingerprint
nodes.summary: Builds a hashed composite fingerprint from machine, OS, and CI environment signals so events can be coarsely deduplicated without exposing PII.

nodes.name: _log_failure
nodes.summary: Writes a scrubbed failure breadcrumb to the rotating analytics error log and forwards a sanitized exception to Sentry when configured.

nodes.name: _write_text_atomic
nodes.summary: Writes text atomically to a target path via tmp-file rename plus parent-dir fsync to survive interrupted writes on crash or SIGKILL.

nodes.name: _file_lock
nodes.summary: Context manager that acquires an exclusive file lock with a bounded retry/backoff window so concurrent CLI processes serialize anonymous-ID writes.

nodes.name: _rotate_event_log
nodes.summary: Rotates the local PostHog debug event log when it exceeds the configured line cap, keeping disk usage bounded for opt-in event tracing.

nodes.name: _coerce_properties
nodes.summary: Validates and coerces caller-supplied event properties into the supported PropertyValue scalar set, dropping or flagging invalid values.

nodes.name: capture_install_detected_if_needed
nodes.summary: Emits the install_detected event exactly once per machine using a touch-once marker, so install metrics are not double-counted across CLI runs.

nodes.name: auth.py
nodes.summary: LangGraph multi-tenant authentication module that verifies JWTs and enforces per-org access on threads, assistants, and crons via Auth handlers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses LangGraph SDK's @auth.on.* decorator pattern to attach per-resource authorization filters keyed off the user's org_id.

nodes.name: authenticate
nodes.summary: LangGraph @auth.authenticate handler that validates the bearer JWT, maps verification errors to HTTP 401/403, and returns the minimal user dict.

nodes.name: on_thread_create
nodes.summary: Authorization hook that scopes thread-create requests to the caller's org_id by injecting a metadata filter.

nodes.name: __main__.py
nodes.summary: OpenSRE CLI entry point that loads dotenv, registers Click commands, installs SIGINT and questionary handlers, captures invocation analytics, and dispatches the CLI.
nodes.languageNotes: Defers analytics capture until after Click resolves the command tree so events record the actual invoked subcommand path.

nodes.name: cli
nodes.summary: Root Click group that renders the landing screen, installs the CLI version flag, and threads invocation-analytics state through the Click context.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Process-level main that initializes Sentry, runs the CLI, captures unhandled exceptions, flushes analytics, and translates errors into appropriate exit codes.

nodes.name: _resolve_command_parts
nodes.summary: Walks the Click command tree to translate raw argv into the actual nested command path, skipping option values to avoid leaking user data into analytics.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: CLI commands barrel that imports each subcommand module and exposes register_commands to attach them to the root Click group.

nodes.name: agent.py
nodes.summary: CLI command group `agents` providing list/register/forget subcommands for managing agent registrations from the terminal.

nodes.name: deploy.py
nodes.summary: Interactive `deploy` CLI group orchestrating Railway, LangSmith, and EC2 deployment flows with health checks, branch prompting, and remote URL persistence.

nodes.name: _run_deploy_interactive
nodes.summary: Drives the interactive deploy wizard, prompting for the deployment style and branch, dispatching to Railway/LangSmith/EC2 backends, and surfacing health status afterwards.

nodes.name: deploy_ec2
nodes.summary: EC2 redeploy subcommand that loads existing config, validates bundling, and triggers the EC2 deployment operation with health polling.

nodes.name: deploy_langsmith
nodes.summary: LangSmith/LangGraph deploy subcommand that builds the remote URL, persists it, and triggers the LangSmith deployment method.

nodes.name: deploy_railway
nodes.summary: Railway deploy subcommand wrapping the Railway CLI deployment method and persisting the resulting remote URL.

nodes.name: doctor.py
nodes.summary: `opensre doctor` command that audits Python version, .env presence, LLM provider config, integrations, and CLI freshness, then renders a status report.

nodes.name: _render_doctor_results
nodes.summary: Renders the doctor diagnostics report as a Rich table with per-check status, advice, and an aggregated exit code suggestion.

nodes.name: _check_llm_provider
nodes.summary: Doctor check that probes configured LLM providers via the llm_cli registry to verify API keys and connectivity.

nodes.name: doctor_command
nodes.summary: Click command entry point for `opensre doctor` that executes all checks and exits with a code reflecting overall health.

nodes.name: general.py
nodes.summary: Top-level CLI commands package providing version, update, uninstall, health, and the primary `investigate` command that runs the agent against an alert.

nodes.name: investigate_command
nodes.summary: `opensre investigate` Click command that builds the alert payload, captures investigation analytics, runs the agent locally, and renders results or service-level summaries.

nodes.name: _run_service_investigation
nodes.summary: Runs an investigation scoped to a named service by synthesizing an alert from templates and dispatching it through the runtime alert pipeline.

nodes.name: health_command
nodes.summary: `opensre health` Click command that runs local health checks across configured integrations and prints a concise status view.

nodes.name: update_command
nodes.summary: `opensre update` Click command that captures lifecycle analytics and delegates to the support update routine to upgrade the installed CLI.

nodes.name: integrations.py
nodes.summary: `integrations` CLI group exposing setup, list, show, remove, and verify subcommands that delegate to the integrations.cli backend.

nodes.name: setup_integration
nodes.summary: `integrations setup` subcommand that captures setup analytics and runs the integration configuration wizard for a chosen integration.

nodes.name: verify_integration
nodes.summary: `integrations verify` subcommand that exercises the integration's verification path and reports success or actionable failure.

nodes.name: remote.py
nodes.summary: Hosted-runtime `remote` CLI group orchestrating preflight checks, interactive browsing, ops (status/logs/restart), streamed and blocking investigations, and pull/import flows.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest CLI module — coordinates remote client, ops adapter, and renderer layers behind a single Click group with many sub-flows.

nodes.name: _run_remote_interactive
nodes.summary: Drives the interactive remote menu — picking a remote, running preflight, and dispatching to deploy, browse, ops, or investigate flows.

nodes.name: _browse_investigations
nodes.summary: Paginates remote investigations in an interactive picker so operators can drill into prior runs from the terminal.

nodes.name: _run_streamed_investigation

layers.name: CLI Surface
layers.description: User-facing Click CLI, onboarding wizard, interactive TTY shell (with the new command_registry, action_executor/planner and execution_policy modules), investigation runner, local-LLM helpers and command-support utilities under app/cli/.

layers.name: Runtime Core
layers.description: Application composition root: main.py, webapp.py, config/strict_config, llm_credentials, output, version, the LangGraph entry shim, plus shared state, types, constants, the pipeline graph/runner and SDK/MCP entrypoints.

layers.name: LangGraph Nodes
layers.description: Per-stage LangGraph nodes orchestrating the investigation pipeline: alert extraction, window adaptation, planning, action execution, root-cause diagnosis, parallel execution, post-processing and publishing.

layers.name: Agent Tools
layers.description: Auto-discovered tool registry and per-tool packages exposed to the agent (Datadog, Grafana, GitHub, S3, Lambda, Sentry, Vercel, Kubernetes, Snowflake, VictoriaLogs, CloudOpsBench K8s, etc.) plus shared tool decorators, base classes and helpers.

layers.name: Integration Layer
layers.description: Vendor integration configuration, normalization, validation, verification, selectors and the catalog/store, plus subprocess-backed LLM CLI adapters under app/integrations/llm_cli/.

layers.name: Vendor Service Clients
layers.description: Reusable HTTP/API clients and adapters used by tools and nodes (Datadog, Grafana, AlertManager, ArgoCD, Coralogix, EKS, LLM client, Snowflake, VictoriaLogs, etc.), plus auth (JWT/JWKS), sandboxed runner, alert normalization and PostHog analytics.

layers.name: Cross-Cutting Concerns
layers.description: Utilities, delivery channels (Slack/Discord/Telegram), env/sentry/OTLP setup and truncation helpers, sensitive-data masking, and the guardrails engine/audit/rules used across the agent.

layers.name: Remote Hosted Runtime
layers.description: Hosted runtime surface: FastAPI server, streaming, Vercel poller, Slack/Discord context handlers, system metrics and ops provider hooks under app/remote/.

layers.name: Deployment & Infrastructure
layers.description: Deployment methods (Railway, LangSmith) and runtime operations (health, EC2 config, provider validation), root-level Dockerfiles, install scripts, Makefile, plus docker-compose stacks and the install-proxy worker under infra/.

layers.name: Project Meta & CI/CD
layers.description: GitHub Actions workflows, issue/PR templates, dependabot/CodeQL configs, root-level project config (pyproject.toml, mypy.ini, pytest.ini, ruff.toml, langgraph.json, .env.example), top-level docs (README, AGENTS, CLAUDE, SETUP, DEPLOYMENT) and knowledge-graph metadata.

tour.title: Project Overview & Repo Map
tour.description: Start here. README.md introduces OpenSRE as an open-source SRE agent that connects 60+ observability tools and runs LangGraph-driven investigations from raw alert to root cause. AGENTS.md is the contributor's repo map: it lays out app/, tests/, and docs/, and tells you which paths back the CLI, pipeline, nodes, tools, and integrations layers you will visit in the next steps. Read these together to build a mental model before opening any code.

tour.title: How `opensre` Boots: CLI Entry & Click Commands
tour.description: Running the `opensre` console script lands in app/cli/__main__.py, which loads dotenv, registers Click commands, installs SIGINT handlers, and dispatches subcommands like `investigate`, `chat`, and `wizard`. app/main.py is the legacy CLI entrypoint that still wires alert template loading, payload parsing, and Sentry SDK setup, and pyproject.toml is where the `opensre` console_script gets bound to these modules. This is the boundary between the OS shell and the agent runtime.
tour.languageLesson: Python `console_scripts` entry points (declared in pyproject.toml) generate small launcher executables on `pip install` that import the named callable. This is how `opensre` becomes a first-class command without committing a bash wrapper.

tour.title: The Interactive Shell: Loop, Commands, and Action Pipeline
tour.description: When the user types `opensre` with no subcommand, they drop into the prompt-toolkit REPL built in app/cli/interactive_shell/loop.py. The recently refactored shell is layered: command_registry/__init__.py composes per-domain slash-command modules into one dispatcher; action_planner.py turns natural-language input into a deterministic list of PlannedActions; action_executor.py runs those actions (shell commands, sample alerts, synthetic tests); and execution_policy.py + execution_tier.py gate every action through an EXEMPT/SAFE/ELEVATED tier with allow/ask/deny semantics. This planner -> policy -> executor split is the heart of the new shell.
tour.languageLesson: Python StrEnum (used by ExecutionTier) gives you string-valued enum members that compare equal to their string form — convenient for serialization across CLI/JSON boundaries while still preserving the tight type checking of an Enum.

tour.title: Shell Grounding: Docs Reference, Diagnostics, and the LangGraph-Free Agent
tour.description: The interactive shell augments the LLM with two grounding sources: docs_reference.py fingerprints and ranks docs/*.mdx pages so the assistant can cite the project's own documentation, and grounding_diagnostics.py logs cache hit/miss telemetry when TRACER_VERBOSE=1. cli_agent.py is the lightweight conversational assistant that streams Markdown answers without spinning up the full LangGraph — it is the fast path for chat, separate from the heavyweight investigation pipeline you'll meet in Step 5. session.py holds the ReplSession state container that ties the latest run, telemetry counters, and task registry together across turns.

tour.title: From `opensre investigate` to the LangGraph
tour.description: Once the user runs an investigation, control flows through app/cli/investigation/__init__.py (the barrel exposing run_investigation_cli), payload.py (which loads alert payloads from file/stdin/prompt and parses them into a typed structure), and investigate_input.py (questionary-driven interactive selection). These modules normalize the alert and hand it to the pipeline. app/graph_pipeline.py is a compatibility shim that re-exports build_graph from the canonical location, app/pipeline/graph.py — preserve the shim, but read graph.py for the real wiring.

tour.title: The LangGraph: Pipeline Assembly, Routing, and State
tour.description: app/pipeline/graph.py is the architectural centerpiece — it constructs the StateGraph, registers every node (auth, chat, extract_alert, plan_actions, execute_actions, root_cause_diagnosis, publish_findings), and wires their edges. routing.py holds the conditional predicates that decide chat-vs-investigation and when to loop the planner. app/state/__init__.py (fan-in 21 — one of the most-imported modules in the repo) defines AgentState, AgentStateModel, ChatMessage, and the factories every node reads from and writes to. Once you understand this triple, the rest of the codebase reduces to 'a node that mutates state'.
tour.languageLesson: LangGraph StateGraph models a workflow as nodes that read/write a shared TypedDict-like state. Conditional edges return the *name* of the next node, which makes routing declarative and unit-testable — you can exercise routing.py predicates without instantiating the full graph.

tour.title: A Representative Node: Alert Extraction & Action Planning
tour.description: extract_alert/extract_node.py is the first investigation node — it reads the raw payload from state, optionally calls the LLM with the structured-output models defined in models.py to parse free-form alerts into a normalized AlertExtraction, and emits a problem statement. plan_actions.py then seeds, prioritizes, and rebalances the investigation plan based on detected sources and prior evidence. Together these show the canonical node shape used across app/nodes/: read state, call LLM/tools, write state. publish_findings/node.py is the terminal counterpart that renders the final report and dispatches it to delivery channels.
tour.languageLesson: Pydantic structured-output models (AlertExtraction in models.py) double as both the LLM response_format schema and the runtime validator. The same class enforces shape on the wire and gives you typed attribute access in Python — one source of truth for both contracts.

tour.title: Tool Registry: The Auto-Discovered Plugin System
tour.description: Every tool the agent can call lives under app/tools/ and is auto-discovered. base.py defines ToolMetadata (the strict pydantic schema for declarations) and BaseTool, the abstract base class with a fan-in of 22 — the second most-imported file in the repo. tool_decorator.py provides @tool with three overloads (plain, source-bound, fully-configured) for lightweight function-style tools. DataDogLogsTool/__init__.py is a representative tool: it declares input schema, use_cases, and required integrations, then defers HTTP transport to a shared Datadog client. Adding a tool means dropping a module here and letting discovery pick it up.
tour.languageLesson: The @tool decorator pattern with typing.overload signatures lets one decorator name accept three argument shapes while keeping per-shape return types accurate to type checkers. This is how the codebase gives you `@tool`, `@tool(source=...)`, and `@tool(input_schema=..., requires=...)` without three differently-named decorators.

tour.title: Integrations & Onboarding Wizard
tour.description: Integrations are how OpenSRE knows about a user's Datadog, Grafana, Slack, etc. app/cli/wizard/flow.py is the 2000-line interactive onboarding flow that walks new users through provider selection and integration setup, persisting answers to the wizard store. catalog.py is the public facade over _catalog_impl.py (fan-out 21) which classifies, merges, and resolves the effective integration set from env + config. verify.py is the user-facing 'does my integration actually work?' entrypoint backed by _verification_adapters.py (fan-out 28 — the highest in the codebase), which holds per-service verifiers that translate config dicts into live API probes.

tour.title: Service Clients: LLM Multiplexer & Vendor APIs
tour.description: Tools and nodes never call vendor APIs directly — they go through app/services/. llm_client.py is a multi-provider wrapper fronting Anthropic (direct + Bedrock), OpenAI-compatible providers, and others behind one interface, so swapping models is a config change rather than a code change. datadog/client.py is the canonical vendor-client shape: sync and async REST clients for logs, monitors, events, and dashboards, reused by every Datadog tool. guardrails/engine.py wraps LLM input/output: it scans against compiled rules, redacts matches, and raises on policy violations — the safety boundary every agent call passes through.

tour.title: Hosted Runtime: FastAPI, LangGraph Platform, and Docker
tour.description: Beyond the local CLI, OpenSRE ships as a hosted service. app/webapp.py is the FastAPI surface that exposes /health and reports version, graph load status, and LLM config. langgraph.json is the deployment manifest declaring auth.path, http.app, image_distro, and the env file consumed by LangGraph Platform. Dockerfile builds the production container image (langgraph-api stage) used by LangGraph Platform and Railway. .env.example documents the 180+ configuration variables an operator can tune, and DEPLOYMENT.md ties the whole story together with operator-facing deployment runbooks.
tour.languageLesson: LangGraph deployment manifests (langgraph.json) declare the dotted import path of your compiled graph (`http.app`) and let the platform autoscale a stateless API around it. The same `build_graph()` you read in Step 6 powers both `opensre investigate` locally and the hosted runtime — one graph, two surfaces.

tour.title: CI/CD, Tests, and Quality Gates
tour.description: Closing the loop: .github/workflows/ci.yml is the primary CI workflow running lint, typecheck, format-check, and the unit/coverage suite on every PR — the same gates AGENTS.md tells you to run locally with `make check`. release.yml builds, tags, and publishes opensre on push to main. The Makefile is the canonical task runner for the 99 targets covering install, test, verify, deploy, and cleanup — `make test-cov` and `make verify-integrations` are the two commands you will run most often. Skim SETUP.md for the dev-environment quick start and you are ready to contribute.
tour.languageLesson: Makefile targets that aren't backed by a real file should be declared `.PHONY` so make never tries to satisfy them by checking timestamps. This repo's Makefile chains quality gates (`check: lint typecheck format-check test-cov`) so one command exercises every gate CI runs.
